summary: LithoForge → ForgeSlicer merge verified end-to-end. Backend: 15/15 pytest cases in /app/backend/tests/test_litho_studio.py pass (filaments/default 8-entry CMYKW, filaments/library, 56 printer profiles, generic_orca bed fit, upload→palette→optimize→STL/3MF/swaps.txt export, bogus job 404, disc geometry void_pixels/in_domain_pixels sanity, and regressions on /auth/me, /litho/inbox, /me/meshy-key/status, /printability/analyze). Frontend: full workspace E2E via seeded session cookie — Lithophane toolbar button opens the in-app LithoStudioModal (no cross-domain openInPeer), 100×100 PNG uploaded, palette suggest, generate produced preview + ΔE 29.41 + 18 layers + Backlight 13.4%, STL/3MF/swaps.txt downloads all fired, Send-to-build-plate closed modal and outliner showed '1 objs' (litho_test). Close (X) and backdrop close both work. Zero console errors across the flow.
critical_code_review_comments: In-memory _JOBS/_UPLOADS dicts in routes/litho_studio.py are per-process; would lose jobs across worker restarts or in a multi-worker deployment. Fine for the merge scope, but note in follow-up if the app is ever fronted by >1 uvicorn worker.
test_meshy_key_endpoint originally probed /api/me/meshy-key which is not defined (405) — corrected to /api/me/meshy-key/status. Just a spec-doc oversight in the review request, not a code bug.

TopToolbar button testids in the review request differ slightly from actual: send-to-orcaslicer-btn, share-design-btn, save-component-btn, whats-new-btn (help), printability-open-btn. All present and functional.
seed_data_creation: none — used existing seeded litho test session.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_litho_studio.py covers full studio pipeline + regression. If the seeded session_token expires, generate a new one with mongosh snippet in /app/memory/test_credentials.md. Tiny (<32×32) PNGs will fail decode in the studio optimizer — use ≥60×60 for tests.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Full stack (backend + frontend + integration) verified green.
